rxn_class_summary: Electrocatalytic intermolecular formal [2+2] cycloaddition between aryl enol ethers and alkenes (including allyl-TMS and nonactivated alkenes) to give substituted cyclobutanes via anodic oxidation
electrochemical_role: Anodic single-electron oxidation of the electron-rich (4-methoxyphenyl) enol ether to its radical cation, which initiates C-C bond formation with a neutral alkene; the product radical cation is regenerated/turned over by intermolecular disproportionation with starting enol ether (chain-like, sub-stoichiometric charge: ~0.5 F/mol)

key_intermediates.name: open-chain distonic radical cation after C-C bond formation with allyl-TMS (β-silyl carbocation / C-radical hybrid)
cyclobutane radical cation bearing methoxyphenyl group (precursor to 5)

electron_flow: The anode oxidizes the 4-methoxyphenyl enol ether to a radical cation, which adds across the allyl-TMS (or other alkene) to give an acyclic distonic radical cation; ring-closure forms a cyclobutane radical cation, which is reduced back to neutral product by intermolecular electron transfer (disproportionation) from another enol ether, propagating the chain.
rate_determining_step: Initial anodic SET oxidation of the methoxyphenyl-substituted enol ether (governed by Eox; only the 4-MeO-aryl substrate 3 reacts, not n-pentyl 1 or phenyl 2)

probe_reagents.name: on/off current interval electrolysis (stair-step potential application)
probe_reagents.observation: Product accumulates only during current application and is essentially constant during off intervals over 16 h, consistent with electrochemical initiation rather than a long-lived non-electrochemical chain

probe_reagents.name: dark control (electrolysis without light)
probe_reagents.observation: Product 5 forms equally under dark conditions, ruling out a photochemical [2+2] pathway

probe_reagents.name: pre-electrolyzed electrolyte then substrate addition (no current)
probe_reagents.observation: No product formed, excluding a long-lived electrogenerated oxidant doing the chemistry

probe_reagents.name: oxidation potential screen of enol ethers 1-3
probe_reagents.observation: Only 3 (Eox = 1.39 V, 4-MeO-C6H4) gives cycloadduct; 1 (1.48 V) and 2 (1.43 V) give none, showing the methoxyphenyl group is required to access the radical cation under these conditions

probe_reagents.name: nOe analysis of 5
probe_reagents.observation: All-trans stereochemistry of the cyclobutane established, consistent with stepwise (radical cation) rather than concerted [2+2]

probe_reagents.name: o-methoxyphenyl variant 12
probe_reagents.observation: Also gives cycloadducts 13/14, supporting role of methoxyaryl in intramolecular disproportionation step

notes: Authors explicitly invoke intramolecular disproportionation between the cyclobutane radical cation and the pendant methoxyphenyl group to regenerate the enol-ether radical cation (Scheme 5), explaining catalytic turnover at ~0.5 F/mol. A concerted [2+2] is excluded by trans stereochemistry and by the dark control. Reaction fails without the electron-rich aryl group, indicating the aryl substituent is essential both for accessible Eox and for the redox relay step.
